name: Blue Ribbon Crab Quesadillas
recipeIngredient: 1 medium green bell pepper, cut into strips
1/2 medium red bell pepper, cut into strips
1/2 medium yellow bell pepper, cut into strips
1 package 6-inch corn tortillas, or flour tortillas
1 1/2 cups Cheddar cheese, shredded (1/2 cup reserved)
1 1/2 cups Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (1/2 cup reserved)
1 tablespoon olive oil, or vegetable oil
1 small jalapeno pepper, seeded, deveined, and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. In a skillet, heat the oil on medium heat. Add the peppers, onion, and garlic. Cook for 5 to 10 minutes until translucent. Set aside.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine the crabmeat, cilantro, mayonnaise, cayenne pepper, cumin, 1 cup each of the cheeses, jalapeno pepper, and lime juice. Fold gently to avoid breaking the crab meat.
4. Place six tortillas on a large baking sheet. Divide the pepper, onion, and garlic mixture equally among each tortilla. Divide the crabmeat mixture equally among the tortillas. Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top of the crab mixture.
5. Cover each tortilla with another to create a sandwich. Lightly spray the top of each tortilla with cooking spray.
6. Bake in the oven for 7 to 10 minutes.
7. Remove from oven and quarter each tortilla.
8. Garnish with lime slice and serve salsa, sour cream, and guacamole as accompaniments.
